volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 743 - Section 831)

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: ὄμνυ · σὺ δʼ ἔξαρχʼ ὅρκον ὅστις εὐσεβής .
sections.translation: ( to Pylades .) Swear ; begin a pious oath .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: δώσω , λέγειν χρή , τήνδε τοῖσι σοῖς φίλοις .
sections.translation: ( to Pylades .) You must say : I will give this to your friends .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: τοῖς σοῖς φίλοισι γράμματʼ ἀποδώσω τάδε .
sections.translation: I will give this letter to your friends .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: κἀγὼ σὲ σώσω κυανέας ἔξω πέτρας .
sections.translation: And I will see you safely outside the dark rocks .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: τίνʼ οὖν ἐπόμνυς τοισίδʼ ὅρκιον θεῶν ;
sections.translation: What god do you do you swear by , as witness to your oath ?

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: Ἄρτεμιν , ἐν ἧσπερ δώμασιν τιμὰς ἔχω .
sections.translation: Artemis , in whose temple I hold office .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ἄνακτά γʼ οὐρανοῦ , σεμνὸν Δία .
sections.translation: And I swear by the king of heaven , revered Zeus .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἐκλιπὼν τὸν ὅρκον ἀδικοίης ἐμέ ;
sections.translation: But if you leave off your oath , and wrong me ?

sections.label: Section 751
sections.original: ἄνοστος εἴην · τί δὲ σύ , μὴ σῴσασά με ;
sections.translation: May I not return . What about you , if you do not save me ?

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: μήποτε κατʼ Ἄργος ζῶσʼ ἴχνος θείην ποδός .
sections.translation: May I never set foot in Argos alive .

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν ὃν παρήλθομεν λόγον .
sections.translation: Now listen to a consideration we have passed over .

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὖθις ἔσται καινός , ἢν καλῶς ἔχῃ .
sections.translation: But it will be something new , if it is good .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ἐξαίρετόν μοι δὸς τόδʼ , ἤν τι ναῦς πάθῃ ,
sections.translation: Give me this exception : if the ship suffers and the tablet is lost with its cargo in the waves , and I rescue myself only , may this oath not hinder me .

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶσθʼ ὃ δράσω ; πολλὰ γὰρ πολλῶν κυρεῖ ·
sections.translation: Do you know what I will do ? For many attempts hit many targets .

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: τἀνόντα κἀγγεγραμμένʼ ἐν δέλτου πτυχαῖς
sections.translation: I will tell you what is written in the folds of this tablet for you to report to my friends . For this is secure : if you preserve the writing , itself , though silent , will speak its message ; if the writing is lost in the sea ,

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: τὸ σῶμα σῴσας τοὺς λόγους σῴσεις ἐμοί .
sections.translation: when you save yourself , you will save my words .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας τῶν θεῶν ἐμοῦ θʼ ὕπερ .
sections.translation: You have spoken well , on behalf of the gods and me . Make clear to whom I must bring this letter in Argos , and what I must say when I have heard it from you .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: ἄγγελλʼ Ὀρέστῃ , παιδὶ τῷ Ἀγαμέμνονος ·
sections.translation: Report to Orestes , the son of Agamemnon :

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: Ἡ ʼν Αὐλίδι σφαγεῖσʼ ἐπιστέλλει τάδε
sections.translation: the one slain at Aulis sends you this , Iphigenia , who is alive , though no longer alive to those there —

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ἔστʼ ἐκείνη ; κατθανοῦσʼ ἥκει πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Where is she ? Has she come back from the dead ??

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἣν ὁρᾷς σύ · μὴ λόγοις ἔκπλησσέ με .
sections.translation: The one you are looking at ; don ’ t confuse me by your talk . Bring me to Argos , my brother , before I die .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἐκ βαρβάρου γῆς καὶ μετάστησον θεᾶς
sections.translation: Take me away from the barbarian land and the sacrifices of the goddess , where I hold the office of killing foreigners .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.original: Πυλάδη , τί λέξω ; ποῦ ποτʼ ὄνθʼ ηὑρήμεθα ;
sections.translation: Pylades , what shall I say ? Where have we found ourselves ?

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: ἢ σοῖς ἀραία δώμασιν γενήσομαι .
sections.translation: Or I will be a curse to your house .

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: Ὀρέστα — ;
sections.translation: Orestes ?

sections.label: Section 779b
sections.original: ἵνʼ αὖθις ὄνομα δὶς κλύων μάθῃς .
sections.translation: ( stopping to address Pylades .) So that you may know the name , hearing it twice .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ὦ θεοί .
sections.translation: O gods !

sections.label: Section 780b
sections.original: τί τοὺς θεοὺς ἀνακαλεῖς ἐν τοῖς ἐμοῖς ;
sections.translation: Why do you invoke the gods in my affairs ?

sections.label: Section 781
sections.original: οὐδέν · πέραινε δʼ · ἐξέβην γὰρ ἄλλοσε .
sections.translation: No reason ; finish your words ; my thoughts were elsewhere . Perhaps , if I question you , I will not arrive at things I cannot believe .

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: λέγʼ οὕνεκʼ ἔλαφον ἀντιδοῦσά μου θεὰ
sections.translation: Tell him that Artemis saved me , by giving a deer in exchange for me ; my father sacrificed it ,

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: δοκῶν ἐς ἡμᾶς ὀξὺ φάσγανον βαλεῖν ,
sections.translation: thinking that he drove the sword sharply into me ; and she settled me in this land . This is my letter , this is the writing in the tablet .

sections.label: Section 788
sections.original: ὦ ῥᾳδίοις ὅρκοισι περιβαλοῦσά με ,
sections.translation: You have bound me with an easy oath , and sworn very well . I will not take much time

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: τὸν δʼ ὅρκον ὃν κατώμοσʼ ἐμπεδώσομεν .
sections.translation: to carry out the oath I swore .

sections.label: Section 791
sections.original: ἰδού , φέρω σοι δέλτον ἀποδίδωμί τε ,
sections.translation: See , Orestes , I bring you a tablet from your sister here , and give it to you .

sections.label: Section 793
sections.original: δέχομαι · παρεὶς δὲ γραμμάτων διαπτυχὰς
sections.translation: I do receive it , but first I will pass over the letter ’ s folds to take a joy that is not in words .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτη μοι σύγγονʼ , ἐκπεπληγμένος
sections.translation: ( Approaching to embrace Iphigenia .) My dearest sister , with what astonishment and delight I hold you in my unbelieving arms , after learning these marvels !

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: ξένʼ , οὐ δικαίως τῆς θεοῦ τὴν πρόσπολον
sections.translation: Stranger , you are wrongly defiling the attendant of the goddess , by putting your hands on her robe that should not be touched .

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ὦ συγκασιγνήτη τε κἀκ ταὐτοῦ πατρὸς
sections.translation: My own sister , born from my father Agamemnon , do not turn away from me , when you hold your brother and thought you never would !

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: ἐγώ σʼ ἀδελφὸν τὸν ἐμόν ; οὐ παύσῃ λέγων ;
sections.translation: You are my brother ? Stop this talk ! He is well known in Argos and Nauplia .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐκεῖ σός , ὦ τάλαινα , σύγγονος .
sections.translation: Unhappy girl , your brother is not there .

sections.label: Section 806
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἡ Λάκαινα Τυνδαρίς σʼ ἐγείνατο ;
sections.translation: But did Tyndareus ’ daughter , the Spartan , give birth to you ?

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: Πέλοπός γε παιδὶ παιδός , οὗ ʼκπέφυκʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Yes , and my father was Pelops ’ grandson .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ἔχεις τι τῶνδέ μοι τεκμήριον ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? Do you have some proof of this for me ?

sections.label: Section 809
sections.original: ἔχω · πατρῴων ἐκ δόμων τι πυνθάνου .
sections.translation: I do ; ask me something about our father ’ s home .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: οὐκοῦν λέγειν μὲν χρὴ σέ , μανθάνειν δʼ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Well , it is for you to speak , for me to learn .

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: λέγοιμʼ ἄν , ἀκοῇ πρῶτον Ἠλέκτρας τάδε ·
sections.translation: I will say first what I have heard from Electra . Do you know of the strife that was between Atreus and Thyestes ?

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: ἤκουσα · χρυσῆς ἀρνὸς ἦν νείκη πέρι .
sections.translation: I have heard of it ; the quarrel concerned a golden ram .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: ταῦτʼ οὖν ὑφήνασʼ οἶσθʼ ἐν εὐπήνοις ὑφαῖς ;
sections.translation: Did you not weave these things in a fine-textured web ?

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἐγγὺς τῶν ἐμῶν κάμπτεις φρενῶν .
sections.translation: O dearest , you are bending your course near to my heart !

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: εἰκώ τʼ ἐν ἱστοῖς ἡλίου μετάστασιν ;
sections.translation: And the image of the sun in the middle of the loom ?

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: ὕφηνα καὶ τόδʼ εἶδος εὐμίτοις πλοκαῖς .
sections.translation: I wove that shape also , in fine threads .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: καὶ λούτρʼ ἐς Αὖλιν μητρὸς ἀνεδέξω πάρα ;
sections.translation: And you received a ceremonial bath from your mother , for Aulis ?

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: οἶδʼ · οὐ γὰρ ὁ γάμος ἐσθλὸς ὤν μʼ ἀφείλετο .
sections.translation: I know ; for no happy marriage has taken that memory from me .

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τί γάρ ; κόμας σὰς μητρὶ δοῦσα σῇ φέρειν ;
sections.translation: What about this ? You gave locks of your hair to be brought to your mother ?

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: μνημεῖά γʼ ἀντὶ σώματος τοὐμοῦ τάφῳ .
sections.translation: As a memorial , in place of my body , in the tomb .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: ἃ δʼ εἶδον αὐτός , τάδε φράσω τεκμήρια ·
sections.translation: What I myself have seen , I will say for proof : an old spear of Pelops , in my father ’ s house , which he brandished in his hand when he won Hippodamia ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: ἐκτήσαθʼ Ἱπποδάμειαν , Οἰνόμαον κτανών ,
sections.translation: the maiden of Pisa , and killed Oenomaus ; it was hung up in your rooms .

sections.label: Section 827
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , οὐδὲν ἄλλο , φίλτατος γὰρ εἶ ,
sections.translation: O dearest , for you are my dearest , none other , I have you , Orestes ,

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: Ἀργόθεν , ὦ φίλος .
sections.translation: far from our country of Argos , my darling .

sections.label: Section 831
sections.original: κἀγώ σε τὴν θανοῦσαν , ὡς δοξάζεται .
sections.translation: And I have you , who were thought to be dead . Tears , and laments mixed with joy , fill your eyes and also mine .
